title: Boundary Conditions Theory
subtitle: BCs for Tube Flow with R410A Two-Phase Evaporation

part1.title: Core Theory - Boundary Condition Fundamentals
topics: What are boundary conditions and why they matter
Types of boundary conditions: Dirichlet, Neumann, Robin
Physical interpretation: fixed value, fixed gradient, mixed

part2.title: Physical Challenge - BC Selection for Evaporator Tube
topics: Inlet BC: Subcooled liquid R410A entry
Outlet BC: Handling reverse flow and pressure specification
Wall BC: No-slip, fixed temperature vs fixed heat flux
Axis BC: Symmetry for 2D axisymmetric formulation

part3.title: Architecture & Implementation - OpenFOAM fvPatchField Framework
topics: Basic BC implementations: fixedValue, fixedGradient, zeroGradient
Derived BC: inletOutlet for flux-based switching

part4.title: Quality Assurance - BC Verification and Debugging
topics: Verifying BC implementation in source code
Common BC mistakes and how to debug
Testing BCs for R410A evaporator case

critical_notes: fvPatchField inherits from Field<Type>, not a separate hierarchy
Mixed BC formula uses valueFraction for weighting (0=gradient, 1=value)
inletOutlet switches based on flux sign (positive=outflow/zeroGrad, negative=inflow/fixedValue)

learning_objectives: Understand the mathematical classification of boundary conditions (Dirichlet, Neumann, Robin)
Explain the physical meaning of fixed value, fixed gradient, and mixed BCs
Select appropriate BCs for R410A evaporator tube simulation
Implement and verify boundary conditions in OpenFOAM case files

connections_to_other_days.from_day_02: Builds on Gauss divergence theorem and face integrals
connections_to_other_days.from_day_05: Requires understanding of mesh topology (points, faces, cells, patches)
connections_to_other_days.to_day_13: BC specification affects geometry and mesh generation decisions
connections_to_other_days.to_day_21: BCs are critical for pressure-velocity coupling implementation
connections_to_other_days.to_day_50: VOF interface requires careful BC specification at walls

inlet.reason: Known inlet velocity for liquid R410A

outlet.reason: Zero gradient for outflow, fixed zero for reverse flow

wall_T.reason: Either fixed wall temperature or constant heat flux

concept_check: Explain why boundary conditions are necessary for well-posed PDE problems
Compare Dirichlet (fixed value) vs Neumann (fixed gradient) boundary conditions
Derive the mixed (Robin) BC formula from weighted combination
Explain how inletOutlet switches between BC modes based on flux
code_verification: Locate fvPatchField base class in source code
Verify mixed BC evaluate() formula matches mathematical derivation
Trace how inletOutlet determines flux direction
Find the deltaCoeffs() function and explain its purpose
practical_application: Specify complete BC set for R410A evaporator tube (U, p, T, alpha)
Convert between fixed temperature and fixed heat flux wall BCs
